subject: pothole on Rice Ave near 25th is getting worse
body_clean: mayor just a heads up that pothole at rice and 25th is getting real bad. ran over it yesterday and felt like i lost a hubcap. been there since at least february. somebody's gonna blow a tire. any chance public works can patch it before it eats someone's rim whole?

subject: pothole on Rice Ave near 25th is getting worse
body_clean: Ray — logged Rice Ave/25th as a priority patch. Crew is running cold-mix fills this week; that intersection is on the list for Wednesday. Warm-mix resurfacing of that block segment is in the May window. Thanks for flagging it.

subject: Faded crosswalk stripes on Washington Blvd at 50th Ave
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor Okonkwo, I walk my grandchildren to the library every Tuesday along Washington Blvd and the crosswalk at 50th Avenue has nearly disappeared. Drivers treat it like open pavement. It's a safety concern, especially with summer foot traffic. Could the village arrange restriping before school starts? Thank you for your time.

subject: Faded crosswalk stripes on Washington Blvd at 50th Ave
body_clean: Ms. Kosch, thank you for writing. I've forwarded your concern to Public Works, who has added the Washington Blvd/50th Ave crosswalk to the summer thermoplastic striping schedule, anticipated before August 15th. We appreciate you looking out for pedestrian safety in Bellwood.

subject: Stop sign down on Bohland Ave at Granville
body_clean: Mayor, the stop sign at Bohland Ave and Granville Ave has been knocked down. The post is bent and the sign is lying in someone's parkway. I went through that intersection this morning and almost got T-boned because nobody was stopping. This needs same-day attention.

subject: Stop sign down on Bohland Ave at Granville
body_clean: Mr. Ruiz — crew is en route now. We keep spare sign stock and a post-driver on the service truck for exactly this. Bohland/Granville should have a temporary stop restored within the hour and a permanent post set by end of day. If you see it still down after noon, call us at 708-555-0141.

subject: Stop sign down on Bohland Ave at Granville
body_clean: Drove past at lunchtime — new sign is up and the post looks solid. Really appreciate the fast turnaround. That's exactly what we need.

subject: Sinkhole forming on Eastern Ave near Monroe St
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, there is a growing depression in the pavement on Eastern Ave about halfway between Monroe St and Madison St. It started as a small dip but over two weeks it has noticeably sunk and the asphalt around the edges is cracking in a ring pattern. I'm worried this is a sinkhole over a failing pipe. It's nearly a lane wide now — please have someone look at this soon.

subject: Sinkhole forming on Eastern Ave near Monroe St
body_clean: Ms. Subramaniam — I had crew out there this morning. There's a real void forming; we placed barricades and flashing lights around it and called Verdi Excavating to camera-inspect the storm sewer lateral beneath. We should know by Thursday whether it's a collapsed pipe or settling fill. I'll keep the Mayor's office updated. Thanks for the early heads-up.

subject: Repave the 1900 block of Marshall Ave — long overdue
body_clean: Mayor, I've lived on the 1900 block of Marshall Ave for over twenty years. This street has not seen a real resurfacing since at least 2010. Patching on top of patches, rough ridges where overlays don't match, curb lips so high they scrape low-profile tires. Is there any chance this block makes the capital paving list this year or next? Happy to bring neighbors together if that helps make the case.

subject: Repave the 1900 block of Marshall Ave — long overdue
body_clean: Marcus, thanks for writing and for your patience. I asked Frank DiMeo to pull the pavement condition index for the 1900 block of Marshall, and it's near the top of our unfunded resurfacing backlog. I can't promise this construction season, but I want to see it in the next capital budget cycle. I'll be in touch once we have a firmer picture — really appreciate you staying engaged.

subject: big pothole Washington Blvd and Bellwood Ave corner
body_clean: yo mayor that corner at washington and bellwood ave is rough. pothole is like a foot across on the southbound approach. been two weeks since i reported it to the hotline. nothing happened. just checking if anyone got it

subject: Crosswalk at St. Charles Rd and Frederick Ave badly faded
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, the painted crosswalk at St. Charles Road and Frederick Avenue is almost invisible. I cross there with my kids to get to the bus stop and cars just don't yield anymore — I think because they literally can't see the markings. School just started so timing is really important. Could you please put this on the restriping schedule?

subject: Potholes on Hirsch Ave between 19th and 25th — had to get alignment checked
body_clean: Dear Mayor, Hirsch Avenue between 19th Ave and 25th Ave is in terrible condition after this past winter. I've counted at least eight potholes, some quite deep. Had my alignment checked after hitting them last month — wasn't cheap. This is a cut-through street and people drive it at speed. Would appreciate the paving attention. Thank you.

subject: Missing stop sign — Harvard Ave at St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Good morning. I'm emailing the Mayor directly because I'm not sure who else to contact. The stop sign at Harvard Ave and St. Paul Ave is completely gone — not knocked sideways, just gone, post and all. I watched three cars roll through this morning without stopping. Someone is going to get hurt. Please send someone out today.

subject: Missing stop sign — Harvard Ave at St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Mr. Warrick — crew dispatched immediately. We'll have a temporary sign up within the hour and a permanent post set today. Mayor Okonkwo flagged this to me as top priority the moment your email came in. We're also checking the area for any other vandalized signs. Thank you for calling it in right away.

subject: Sinkhole forming in front of 612 Morris Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, there is an alarming depression in the street directly in front of 612 Morris Ave. It appeared last week and got noticeably larger after Tuesday's rain. The surface feels soft when you walk over it. I'm worried this could collapse suddenly. Can someone from the village inspect it quickly?

subject: pothole cluster on 25th Ave south of St Charles
body_clean: mayor three or four potholes bunched together on 25th ave just south of st charles. in the right lane. been there since end of september. one of them is deep enough to jolt you good. can public works do a patch run down there soon

subject: 400 block of Englewood Ave — please add to resurfacing list
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing on behalf of several households on the 400 block of Englewood Ave. The surface has deteriorated past the point of effective patching — you can see seams of old overlays from the 1990s and new cracking is opening around them. Three neighbors have damaged rims or tires this year. Is there a process for making a formal request to be included in a capital resurfacing project?

subject: Faded pedestrian crossing on Madison St at 19th Ave — Metra commuters use this daily
body_clean: Hello Mayor — the crosswalk striping on Madison Street at 19th Avenue is almost entirely gone. It's a busy pedestrian route to the Metra UP-W station in the morning rush. I've nearly been clipped twice stepping into the street because drivers clearly don't register there's a crosswalk anymore. A fresh coat of thermoplastic would go a long way.

subject: Deep pothole on Bellwood Ave just north of the Metra tracks
body_clean: Dear Mayor, there is a very deep pothole on Bellwood Avenue just north of the UP-W Metra tracks. A lot of heavy equipment crosses there during maintenance and I think it opened the pavement up. My car dropped into it last Thursday and I'm fairly sure I bent a rim. Commuters drive this route constantly on the way to the station. Can it get fixed soon?

subject: Stop sign knocked sideways at Hirsch Ave and 50th Ave
body_clean: Hello Mayor, the stop sign at the northwest corner of Hirsch Avenue and 50th Avenue is knocked over — it looks like it got hit sometime overnight. The sign is hanging off the post at a steep angle and is not visible to drivers at all. This is a school route and kids will be walking it in a few weeks when fall semester starts. Please get a repair crew out as soon as possible.
